task: Find press releases by the antitrust division in 2022.
thoughts: I can see the Justice Department homepage. Let me click on "News" to find the press releases section.
I can see the News dropdown menu with "Press Releases" option. Let me click on it.
I can see the Press Releases page. It says press releases published before January 20, 2025 can be found in the Archives. Since I need 2022 press releases, let me click on the "Archives" link.

I can see the Press Releases Archive page with filters. I need to:
1. Filter by Year (2022)
2. Filter by Component (Antitrust Division)

Let me first click on "Filter by Year" to expand it.
I can see the year filter expanded with 2022 (1388) as an option. Let me check the 2022 checkbox.
Let me click more precisely on the 2022 checkbox.

2022 is now checked (showing 1388 results). I can see the month sub-filters expanded. Now I need to also filter by the Antitrust Division component. Let me scroll down to find the "Filter by Component" section.
I can see 1388 results for 2022 and I can also see the "Filter by Component" section at the bottom. Let me click on it to expand it and find the Antitrust Division.
The "Filter by Component" section is expanding. Let me scroll down to see the component options.
I can see the "Filter by Component" section is now expanded and I can see "Antitrust Division (62)" as the first option. Let me click on it to filter by the Antitrust Division.
It looks like the Filter by Component collapsed. Let me click on it again to expand it.
Now I can see the "Filter by Component" section expanded with "Antitrust Division (62)" as the first option. Let me click the checkbox next to it.
It seems the filter keeps toggling. Let me click the "+" icon to expand the Filter by Component section.
Now I can see the component filter is expanded with "Antitrust Division (62)" visible. Let me click on the checkbox next to "Antitrust Division (62)".
The filter keeps collapsing. Let me try expanding it and then clicking specifically on the "Antitrust Division" text rather than the checkbox area.
Good, the Filter by Component is now expanded and I can see "Antitrust Division (62)". Now let me click directly on the "Antitrust Division (62)" text/label to check it.
The filter is collapsed. Let me try a different approach and navigate directly using URL parameters to filter by the Antitrust Division and year 2022.
The URL approach didn't apply the filters. Let me scroll to the top of the page and try a different approach - first expand the component filter, then quickly click the Antitrust checkbox.
Let me try a different approach. I'll use the keyword search with "antitrust" and set the date range to 2022.
Let me try a different approach. I'll use the keyword search with "antitrust" and set the date range to 2022.
The text didn't seem to go into the field. Let me click directly on the keyword search input field and type.

Now I can see the typeable fields marked in orange. I can see the Keyword Search input field, Start Date, and End Date fields. Let me click on the Keyword Search input field and type "antitrust division", then set the dates.
Let me scroll down slightly and get a better view with the grid to precisely click the keyword search field.

Now I can see the grid and the typeable fields clearly. The Keyword Search input field is at approximately (600, 690). The Start Date field is around (840, 690) and End Date is around (1010, 690). Let me click precisely on the Keyword Search input field center.
Let me try a fresh approach and navigate directly to the Antitrust Division's press releases page.
I'm now on the Antitrust Division's Press Releases page. I can see the "Filter by Year" option on the left side. Let me click on it to expand it and select 2022.
The Filter by Year section is now expanded showing 2026 (10) and 2025 (49). I need to scroll down in the filter to see 2022. Let me scroll down in the sidebar to find 2022.
This page only shows post-January 2025 results. The 2022 press releases are in the archives. Let me try navigating to the archived Antitrust Division press releases.

I'm back on the Press Releases Archive. Let me try clicking on "Filter by Year" first, then scrolling carefully, and then clicking on the "Antitrust Division" checkbox separately. Let me first click on Filter by Year.
The Filter by Year is expanded. I can see "2022 (1388)". Let me click on the 2022 checkbox first.

Excellent! The 2022 filter is now applied (checked) and the URL has changed to include the facet filter. I can see 1388 results for 2022. Now I notice the URL is `?f%5B0%5D=facet_dates%3A2022`. I need to also add the Antitrust Division component filter. Let me scroll down to find the "Filter by Component" section and click on "Antitrust Division".

I can see the 2022 filter is applied (1388 Results) and I can see "Filter by Component" at the bottom. Now I know the URL pattern uses facet parameters. Let me try to directly navigate to the URL with both the year 2022 and Antitrust Division component filters applied.
The component filter didn't apply. Let me scroll down to expand Filter by Component and click on Antitrust Division with more precision this time.
Good, I can see the 2022 filter is active. Let me scroll down more to see the "Filter by Component" section.

Now I can see the "Filter by Component" with the "+" button. Let me click on it to expand it, then I need to quickly click on "Antitrust Division" before it collapses. Let me first click on the "+" button to expand the component filter.

Now I can see the "Filter by Component" is expanded and "Antitrust Division (62)" with a checkbox is visible at the bottom. Let me click on the "Antitrust Division (62)" checkbox. I can see it's at approximately y=710 with the checkbox at x=171.
